schools.summary: Gill: they had no part in the division of Canaan among the tribes.
Keil & Delitzsch: they shall eat the offerings of the LORD made by fire as their portion.

themes: The priests the Levites... shall have no part nor inheritance with Israel: they shall eat the offerings of the LORD
the ministers fed from the altar
they that serve the altar live of the altar (1 Cor. 9:13-14)

schools.summary: Gill: they had neither field nor vineyard, provision being made for them otherwise.
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD is their inheritance, as he hath said unto them.

themes: the LORD is their inheritance, as he hath said unto them
God himself the portion of his servants
the LORD my portion, therefore will I hope (Lam. 3:24)

schools.summary: Gill: this was due not from the priests but from those who brought the sacrifices, particularly the peace offerings.
Clarke: the word zebach is used of sacrifices.

themes: this shall be the priest's due... the shoulder, and the two cheeks, and the maw
God providing for his ministers by a settled right
the ministry maintained by the people's gifts (Gal. 6:6)

schools.summary: Gill: this is the heave offering, the offering of the firstfruits, the measure fixed by the Jews.
Keil & Delitzsch: the firstfruit of corn, wine, oil, and the first of the fleece.

themes: The firstfruit also of thy corn, of thy wine, and of thine oil, and the first of the fleece
the first and best God's due
Christ the firstfruits (Prov. 3:9; 1 Cor. 15:20)

schools.summary: Gill: God chose the tribe of Levi out of all the other tribes of Israel.
Keil & Delitzsch: chosen to stand and minister in the name of the LORD, he and his sons for ever.

themes: the LORD thy God hath chosen him... to stand to minister in the name of the LORD, him and his sons for ever
the ministry a divine calling
pointing to the eternal High Priest (Heb. 7:24)

schools.summary: Gill: the Levites were dispersed all over the country, employed in teaching the people, and were but sojourners except in their own cities.
Keil & Delitzsch: the Levite coming with all the desire of his mind unto the place which the LORD shall choose.

themes: if a Levite come... with all the desire of his mind unto the place which the LORD shall choose
the welcome of the willing servant
whole-hearted longing to serve, the spirit God loves (Ps. 84:10)

schools.summary: Gill: the Targum reads 'minister in the name of the Word of the Lord' — in the name of Christ, as a type of him, as every priest and sacrifice were.
Keil & Delitzsch: him ministering as his brethren the Levites which stand before the LORD.

themes: he shall minister in the name of the LORD his God, as all his brethren the Levites
the priestly service a type of Christ
the one true Priest in whose name all ministry is done (Heb. 9:24)

schools.summary: Gill: they had equal parts of the sacrifices with the priests who usually ministered there; Jarchi infers they divided the skins and flesh of the sin offerings, beside what came of the sale of his patrimony.

themes: They shall have like portions to eat, beside that which cometh of the sale of his patrimony
a fair and brotherly sharing among ministers
none slighted, all sharing alike (1 Cor. 9:14)

schools.summary: Gill: the land of Canaan often thus described to express God's goodness in bestowing it as a mere favour, typical of the heavenly Canaan, the free gift of God through Christ; they were not to learn the abominations of the nations.

themes: thou shalt not learn to do after the abominations of those nations
a holy separation from heathen wickedness
be not conformed to this world (Rom. 12:2)

schools.summary: Gill: the passing through fire was the service of Molech — the child led by a priest between two fires, devoting him to that deity; divination, observing of times, enchanting, and witchcraft all forbidden.

themes: any one that maketh his son or his daughter to pass through the fire, or that useth divination...
Molech-worship and the occult arts banned
no fellowship with the works of darkness (Eph. 5:11)

schools.summary: Gill: the charmer, by the Targums, binds serpents and scorpions, or says certain words; the consulter with familiar spirits and the necromancer sought the dead; all forbidden.

themes: Or a charmer, or a consulter with familiar spirits, or a wizard, or a necromancer
seeking knowledge from spirits or the dead forbidden
a people should seek unto their God, not the dead (Isa. 8:19)

schools.summary: Gill, with Jarchi: not all these things together but whoever does any of them, and all who consult them; especially abominable in Israel to encourage such; for these God was driving out the nations.

themes: all that do these things are an abomination unto the LORD: and because of these abominations the LORD... doth drive them out
God's abhorrence of the occult
the same sin bringing the same judgment (Lev. 18:24-25)

schools.summary: Gill: sincerely to serve and worship him, faithfully adhere to his word and ordinances, and walk uprightly before him.
Keil & Delitzsch: Israel to be blameless in its intercourse with Jehovah.

themes: Thou shalt be perfect with the LORD thy God
a single-hearted, upright integrity
walk before God and be blameless (Gen. 17:1)

schools.summary: Gill: those nations hearkened to observers of times and diviners and did as they directed.
Keil & Delitzsch: but the LORD thy God hath not suffered thee so to do.

themes: these nations... hearkened unto observers of times, and unto diviners: but... the LORD thy God hath not suffered thee
the privilege of revelation
God making known his will, not by diviners

schools.summary: Gill: not Joshua, Jeremiah, David, nor a succession of prophets, for a single person is spoken of and there is dissimilitude between Moses and any other; but the Messiah, with whom the whole agrees.

themes: The LORD thy God will raise up unto thee a Prophet... like unto me; unto him ye shall hearken
the great Messianic promise of the law
Christ the great Prophet, like Moses (Acts 3:22; John 6:14)

schools.summary: Gill: promised in answer to their request at Horeb, when the law was delivered in so terrible a manner; they had begged not to hear God's voice directly, lest they die.

themes: According to all that thou desiredst of the LORD... in Horeb... Let me not hear again the voice... that I die not
the Prophet answering their plea for a mediator
God speaking to us by his Son (Heb. 1:2)

schools.summary: Gill: the LORD said this unto Moses, who carried their request to him.
Keil & Delitzsch: God's word — they have well spoken that which they have spoken.

themes: They have well spoken that which they have spoken
God approving the desire for a mediator
the way of mediation answered in Christ (1 Tim. 2:5)

schools.summary: Gill: this promise was first made at Sinai, now renewed; God would put his words in the Prophet's mouth.
Keil & Delitzsch: the Prophet from among their brethren, like Moses, with God's words in his mouth.

themes: I will... put my words in his mouth; and he shall speak unto them all that I shall command him
the Prophet the perfect organ of God's word
fulfilled in Christ, speaking the Father's words (John 12:49)

schools.summary: Gill: of whosoever will not hearken to the doctrines of the Gospel, but slights and despises them. Calvin: the commandment to hear God's appointed teacher remains in force even to the end of the world.

themes: whosoever will not hearken unto my words which he shall speak in my name, I will require it of him
to refuse the Prophet is to refuse God
the despiser destroyed (Acts 3:23)

schools.summary: Gill: the prophet who presumes to speak in God's name, pretending a mission he never had, or one speaking in the name of other gods, was to die.

themes: the prophet, which shall presume to speak a word in my name, which I have not commanded... even that prophet shall die
the false prophet condemned
God guarding the truth of his revelation (Jer. 23:31)

schools.summary: Gill: such a thought arises in the mind, a difficulty and query made upon it. Calvin: this exception relates to the prohibition already noticed, God having invested his prophets with authority.

themes: if thou say in thine heart, How shall we know the word which the LORD hath not spoken?
the honest question of discernment
God providing a test to try the spirits (1 John 4:1)

schools.summary: Gill: if the thing does not follow, as the prophecy of Hananiah did not, it is the thing the LORD has not spoken — unless a condition is expressed or implied; they need not be afraid of him.

themes: if the thing follow not, nor come to pass, that is the thing which the LORD hath not spoken
the test of fulfilment unmasking the false
the true word of God stands for ever (Isa. 40:8)
